response_turn1:
Here is my assessment of the retrieved document:

---

LABEL: PARTIAL
CONFIDENCE: 62
REASON: The core drug-interaction content (warfarin + NSAIDs increasing bleeding risk, INR monitoring, risk factors) is consistent with the genuine FDA-approved COUMADIN prescribing information, but the document as presented is a selective, reformatted excerpt that omits large portions of the official label, mixes section numbering inconsistently (§7 Drug Interactions appearing before §5.2 Warnings), and the "Concomitant NSAID Use" subsection heading and its exact wording do not appear verbatim in the official Bristol-Myers Squibb Reference ID 4170204 label, suggesting the text has been paraphrased or lightly edited rather than reproduced faithfully from the authentic source.

---

**Key observations:**

| Element | Assessment |
|---|---|
| Issuing body (FDA / Bristol-Myers Squibb) | Plausible and real |
| Reference ID (4170204) | Consistent with a real BMS filing |
| Revision date (September 2017) | Plausible for COUMADIN labeling |
| Section content on bleeding risk | Broadly accurate |
| Section ordering & formatting | Inconsistent with official FDA label structure |
| "Concomitant NSAID Use" subsection | Not verbatim from verified label text |

**Bottom line:** The document draws on real information but appears to be a summarised or reconstructed version rather than a direct, unaltered reproduction of the official FDA label. I would not rely on it as a verbatim authoritative source; always verify against the current label on [FDA.gov/DailyMed](https://daily
